sections.title: Executive Summary
sections.rendered_content:
This comparison evaluates CEG, GE across their locked annual filing periods, examining revenue scale, profitability, leverage, and cash generation to surface relative positioning within the peer set.

CEG reports $25.5B in revenue (8.3% YoY) and $2.32B in net income for period 2025-12-31. Key profile markers include net margin of 9.1%, debt-to-equity of 0.61x, free cash flow of $1.29B.

GE reports $45.9B in revenue (18.5% YoY) and $8.70B in net income for period 2025-12-31. Key profile markers include net margin of 19.0%, debt-to-equity of 1.08x, free cash flow of $7.26B.

GE leads the peer set in annual revenue, anchoring the scale dimension of the comparison.

GE reports $45.9B in revenue (18.5% YoY) and $8.70B in net income for period 2025-12-31. Key profile markers include net margin of 19.0%, debt-to-equity of 1.08x, free cash flow of $7.26B.

sections.title: Relative Strengths
sections.rendered_content:
### CEG — What Stands Out
- ROE of 16.3% indicates efficient use of shareholder capital.

- Current ratio of 1.53x indicates solid liquidity.
### GE — What Stands Out
- ROE of 45.3% indicates efficient use of shareholder capital.

- Revenue is up 18.5% year over year, showing strong top-line momentum.

sections.title: Risk Comparison
sections.rendered_content:
### Watch Items
- Relative risk is assessed only on surfaced, period-locked peer metrics and does not force false comparability.

- **CEG:** No major quantitative red flag is active in the current annual snapshot.
- **GE:** Significant spike: 934.7% YoY change (2.5σ from mean) (period: 2023-12-31) Significant spike: 798.8% YoY change (2.6σ from mean) (period: 2017-12-31)

sections.title: Analyst Notes
sections.rendered_content:
### Analyst Follow-Up
- Peer conclusions should be read through the locked annual basis, surfaced metric coverage, and explicit comparison-governance policy.

- **CEG:** 2 strength signals and 0 active risk signals in the locked annual basis.
- **GE:** 2 strength signals and 2 active risk signals in the locked annual basis.
- Prioritize next-pass work on margin durability, balance-sheet flexibility, and cash conversion differentials across the peer set.
